title: Tier-Eval v2 Model-Config Manifest Entry
description: One entry in the model-config manifest. Every run-registry row references a manifest entry by `model_config_id`; this schema enumerates what that key resolves to. See TIER-EVAL-V2-SPRINT-PLAN.md §3 (Sprint 0.2) and the source strategy doc §5.3. The manifest is the join target — denormalized fields on registry rows (model_id, quantization, context_limit, sampler_config_id) must be consistent with their manifest entry.

model_config_id.description: Stable key for this combination of model + quantization + sampler + harness + prompt pack. Used as the foreign key from run-registry rows. Suggested format: `<family>-<size>-<quant>-ctx<N>-<sampler_id>-<prompt_pack_id>` (e.g. `qwen36-35b-a3b-q4km-ctx65k-v1prod-pp01`).

model_id.description: Exact model name and revision/hash. For HuggingFace-style refs include the revision (e.g. `Qwen/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B@<git-rev>`). For LiteLLM aliases (e.g. `claw-llama`) include the upstream model + git rev or digest separately.

model_family.description: Family name for aggregation (e.g. `qwen3.6`, `llama3.3`, `mistral-large-2`). Coarse grouping for cross-family comparisons.

parameter_count.description: Nominal parameter count, in number of parameters (not millions/billions). Null if not known or not meaningful (e.g. some MoE configs).

quantization.description: Quantization format and parameters. Examples: `Q4_K_M`, `Q5_K_M`, `Q8_0`, `MLX-4bit`, `fp16`, `bf16`, `none`. Use `none` for unquantized weights.

context_limit.description: Effective context window available to the harness for this config, in tokens. May be smaller than the model's nominal max (e.g. memory budget on tier-16).

runtime_backend.description: Backend/runtime version. Examples: `llama-server@<sha>`, `ollama@0.x.y`, `mlx@a.b.c`, `vllm@a.b.c`. Required even if the harness only supports one backend, so cross-backend comparisons remain unambiguous.

sampler_config_id.description: Stable key for the sampler settings used. The full sampler payload (temperature, top-p, top-k, presence/frequency penalties, repeat penalty, seed policy, min-p, etc.) is stored in `sampler_settings`.

sampler_settings.description: Full sampler settings payload for this config. Optional denormalization — the canonical source can live elsewhere — but recommended for self-contained manifest entries.

seed_policy.description: How the seed is chosen. `task_seeded` is required for paired sampler comparisons.

harness_version.description: Git SHA or release identifier of the test harness at the time this config was registered.

prompt_pack_version.description: System / developer / task prompt pack version. The pack identifies the exact prompt text used for runs against this config.

tier_compatibility.description: Which hardware tiers this config has been observed to run on. Optional — used by the planner to flag impossible (tier, model_config) cells before submitting them to a sweep.

notes.description: Free-form notes (e.g. `replaces qwen35-30b-a3b on tier-64 as of 2026-04-15`).
